title: Bruins call up former 1st-round pick Lukas Reichel on emergency basis
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins recalled Lukas Reichel from Providence on an emergency basis before the game against the Winnipeg Jets.
Reichel, a former 2020 first-round pick, was acquired from Vancouver for a 2026 sixth-round pick.
In three games with Providence, Reichel scored five points, prompting his NHL call-up.

title: World Cup games at Gillette no longer in jeopardy after Foxborough officially approves license
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Foxborough Select Board approved an event license for seven World Cup games at Gillette Stadium after security funding was agreed.
BS26 and Kraft's group committed to covering $7.8 million in security costs, including $1.5 million for safety equipment.
The license includes a traffic plan approved by Massachusetts Department of Transportation and expanded MBTA service for 21,000 patrons per game.

title: 2026 NFL mock draft roundup: Who experts have Patriots selecting with 31st pick
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Mel Kiper Jr. suggests the Patriots draft Texas A&M WR KC Concepcion for their 31st pick to boost offensive potential.
CBS Sports' Mike Renner recommends Georgia LB CJ Allen, highlighting his fit in the Patriots' linebacker needs.
NBC Sports Boston’s Phil Perry proposes Missouri EDGE Zion Young, citing his strong physical play for Patriots’ defense.

title: What Kristaps Porzingis said about his return to TD Garden
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Kristaps Porzingis returned to TD Garden as a Warrior, reflecting on his time with the Celtics and enjoying Boston life.
Porzingis, traded from the Celtics, averages 17.2 points, 4.8 rebounds, and shoots 34.5% from 3-point range for the Warriors.
Warriors coach Steve Kerr praised Porzingis's fit in the team's dynamic, emphasizing his screen ability and shooting touch.

title: Easton police looking for dirt bikers who caused ‘thousands’ in damage at country club
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Easton police are searching for two dirt bikers who trespassed at Easton Country Club, causing extensive damage.
The bikers were last seen entering nearby railroad tracks on Prospect Street after the incident on Sunday.
Police released surveillance images and urge anyone with information to contact Officer Tyler Sutton at 508-230-3322.

title: This Dorchester resident brings trash to City Hall to raise awareness of the litter problem in Boston
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Dorchester resident Alex Alex brings collected city infrastructure and trash to City Hall to highlight Boston's litter issues.
Alex collects items like bus signs and traffic cones, documenting his actions on social media to raise public awareness.
Despite his efforts, city councilors claim to have engaged with him, countering his assertions of being ignored by officials.

title: Celtics’ Derrick White shares beer with Jimmy Fallon, talks dental work on ‘The Tonight Show’
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Derrick White appeared on 'The Tonight Show' with Jimmy Fallon, discussing basketball, fatherhood, and his chipped tooth from the 2024 NBA Finals.
White promoted his new beer collaboration 'White Ale' with Sam Adams, part of the 'Our City. Our Beer' lineup featuring local sports stars.
After a dental mishap during an NBA game, White recounted gluing a loose tooth back in place to finish playing, later visiting the dentist.

title: Nashua, N.H. man arrested after allegedly assaulting 3-month-old son
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Drew Babcock, 36, faces eight counts of first-degree domestic assault after allegedly injuring his 3-month-old son in Nashua.
The child sustained fractures in his ribs, femurs, and clavicle after Babcock allegedly assaulted him, according to court records.
Babcock pleaded not guilty and remains in custody, with a bail hearing scheduled in Hillsborough County Superior Court South.

title: Here’s how much a remote per diem worker makes north of Boston
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Francine, a 66-year-old remote per diem worker, earns $27,000 annually as a senior project manager in clinical research.
Based in Hamilton-Wenham, Francine switched to remote work in 2023 after working in-person for nearly 25 years.
Her major expense is now healthcare costs, and she relies on a mix of pension, 403(b), and salary for financial stability.

title: NTSB: Flight instructor in fatal Mass. plane crash had ‘do not fly’ meds in system
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: NTSB report reveals flight instructor William Hampton had prohibited medications in his system during 2024 Massachusetts plane crash.
Hampton's toxicology report showed presence of Xanax, Ambien, and other CNS depressants, impairing his capacity to fly safely.
Plane crashed near Leyden, MA, killing three, including Fly Lugu Flight School owner; NTSB cites stall mishandling as probable cause.
